role_title: Senior Channel Partner Manager | Venture Capital & Growth Equity
secondary_archetype: Channel Partnerships / BD (VC & Growth Equity focus)

level_signals: Minimum 6 years of work experience required
Expected to 'quarterback the commercial aspects of many of Ramp's most strategic relationships'
Works with 'senior stakeholders across Ramp's Finance, Sales, and Channel organizations'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 6 years of work experience with a strong foundation in Investing (Private Equity / Venture Capital), Management Consulting, Investment Banking, or Investor Relations
Strong discovery skills, with a knack for identifying pain points and consultative selling
History as a top performer, regularly going above and beyond to support commercial outcomes of clients or investments
Dedication to tracking and improving performance and efficiency daily
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with excellent listening skills
Bias for action and a strong desire to work in a fast-paced startup environment
A strong cross-functional collaborator who can build relationships across the company

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in Business Development, Partnerships, or Partner Sales, particularly in roles involving investment firms or financial institutions
Background working at or covering software & payments companies

scope.surface: Financial Institutions Channel — VC & Growth Equity partner program; corporate card and expense management platform distribution
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech; partner-led distribution through VC/PE/accelerator channel
primary_metrics_inferred: Net-new pipeline sourced through VC/PE partners
Senior-level deal influence from investor partners
core_responsibilities: Own the commercial relationships with dozens of leading venture & growth equity firms
Drive revenue for Ramp by sourcing net-new prospective sales opportunities
Identify, build, and nurture relationships with multiple stakeholders within investment funds
Work cross-functionally across Ramp Leadership in Finance, Investor Relations, Sales, Growth, and other functions
Become an expert in Ramp's product, features, and workflows to communicate directly with C-level finance leaders

green_flags: Clear role mandate and ownership area (VC & growth equity channel)
Ramp has significant scale ($100B+ annualized spend, 50,000+ companies)
Cross-functional exposure to Finance, Sales, Investor Relations, and Growth leadership
Benefits package is comprehensive including full medical/dental/vision, 401(k) match, and parental leave
Relocation support to NYC or SF offered
Role involves strategic, high-visibility relationships with leading VC/PE firms
red_flags: Office time requirements and hybrid policy not explicitly defined (relocation support suggests in-person expected)
No equity mentioned explicitly in the JD despite senior-level role at a high-growth fintech

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior', 6+ years requirement, and strategic relationship scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and partner distribution motion)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from relocation support to NYC/SF and in-person engagement emphasis; not explicitly stated)
location.cities_listed (inferred from relocation support to NYC or SF; not explicitly named as role locations)
ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as hybrid or onsite; however, JD mentions relocation support to NYC or SF and emphasis on 'in-person engagements', suggesting physical presence expected. Classified as hybrid.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed anywhere in the JD.
Equity is not mentioned despite this being a senior role at a well-funded fintech startup — may be an omission.
The role covers global benefits (US, Canada, UK) suggesting global hiring eligibility, but the role context (VC/PE firms, US-centric language) implies primary market is US.
